goal: Implement enforceable AI quality thresholds by: (1) adding a `critical` field to eval cases, (2) wiring the existing title-quality and criteria-quality LLM judge prompts into `PromptEvalRunner` so draft-assist cases collect real judge scores, (3) committing the most recent eval output as a regression baseline and creating an `eval-thresholds.json` config, (4) building a threshold enforcement script that reads eval results and compares against the config + baseline, (5) wiring that script into the GitLab `eval:llm-judge` job, and (6) adding unit tests for the enforcement script. The result is a pipeline where deterministic checks hard-gate, judge scores soft-gate with clear pass/fail reporting, and regressions against baseline are detected. Constraints: backend-only for steps 1-3 (no frontend modifications); Java must pass spotlessCheck; preserve existing PromptEvalRunner parameterized test structure; judge scoring is optional (skip if no OPENROUTER_API_KEY); do NOT modify FaithfulnessEvaluator.java; eval results write to backend/build/eval-results/; enforcement script is standalone Node.js (scripts/eval-threshold-check.js) using only Node 20 built-ins.

steps.title: Add `critical` field to EvalCase/EvalResult and mark critical cases in fixture JSON
steps.description:
Add an optional `critical` boolean field to `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/EvalCase.java` record. Jackson 2.18.3 defaults missing primitive record fields to `false`, so no extra annotation is needed beyond the existing `@JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)`.

Update `EvalResult.java` (a class, not a record, with a 5-arg constructor: `(String caseId, String description, String promptVersion, boolean schemaValid, String rawOutput)`) to add `boolean critical` as a 6th constructor parameter. Add a `isCritical()` getter. Include `critical` in the `toString()` output and ensure it is serialized in the JSON report (Jackson will pick up `isCritical()` automatically as a property).

Update all 6 `new EvalResult(...)` call sites in `PromptEvalRunner.java` (one per eval type: commitDraftAssist, commitLint, rcdoSuggest, riskSignal, reconcileAssist, ragQuery) to pass `evalCase.critical()` as the 6th argument.

Update fixture JSON files to mark critical cases:
- `backend/src/test/resources/eval/commit-draft-assist/cases.json` — add `"critical": true` to cases `draft-001`, `draft-004`, `draft-008`
- `backend/src/test/resources/eval/commit-lint/cases.json` — add `"critical": true` to cases `lint-001`, `lint-003`, `lint-004`

Scope: Only modify files in `backend/src/test/`. Do NOT modify any file in `backend/src/main/` or `frontend/`.

steps.title: Wire title-quality and criteria-quality LLM judges into PromptEvalRunner for draft-assist cases
steps.description:
Extend `PromptEvalRunner.evaluateCommitDraftAssist()` to call the title-quality judge and criteria-quality judge when the provider is available and the draft-assist output includes a non-null `suggestedTitle` or `suggestedSuccessCriteria`.

Critical implementation note: `OpenRouterAiProvider.generateSuggestion()` cannot be used for judge calls — it hard-codes its own system prompt and builds its own user message from `AiContext` fields. Since the constraint prohibits modifying `src/main/`, create a private helper method `callJudge(String filledPrompt)` in `PromptEvalRunner` that:
1. Reads `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and `OPENROUTER_MODEL` from env vars
2. Makes a direct HTTP POST to `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions` using `java.net.http.HttpClient`
3. Parses the response to extract the JSON content (strip markdown fences if present)
4. Returns the parsed `JsonNode` scores

Read judge prompt templates from test classpath resources:
- `eval/judge-prompts/title-quality-judge.txt`
- `eval/judge-prompts/criteria-quality-judge.txt`

Substitute placeholders (`{originalTitle}`, `{suggestedTitle}`, `{chessPiece}`, `{commitTitle}`, `{suggestedCriteria}`) and use the filled template as the user message.

Title judge dimensions stored via `addScore()`: `titleJudge_clarity`, `titleJudge_specificity`, `titleJudge_appropriate_scope`, `titleJudge_improvement`, `titleJudge_professional_tone`.

Criteria judge dimensions: `criteriaJudge_measurable`, `criteriaJudge_completeness`, `criteriaJudge_achievable_in_one_week`, `criteriaJudge_testable`.

Guard all judge calls with the existing `providerAvailable` boolean. Wrap in try/catch so judge failures never fail the test.

Scope: Only modify `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/PromptEvalRunner.java`. Do NOT modify any file in `backend/src/main/` or `frontend/`.

steps.title: Commit the most recent eval run as baseline and create eval-thresholds.json
Build the threshold enforcement script
steps.description:
Create `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js` — a standalone Node.js script (no external dependencies beyond Node 20 built-ins) that:

1. Reads `eval-thresholds.json` from the repo root (resolved relative to the script's location via `path.resolve(__dirname, '..')`)
2. Reads the latest `*.json` file from `backend/build/eval-results/` (sorted by filename timestamp, takes most recent)
3. Reads `eval-baseline.json` from the repo root (if it exists — skip regression checks when absent)
4. Computes: `schema_valid_rate`, `overall_case_pass_rate`, `critical_case_pass_rate` (skip if no results have `critical: true`), and mean of each judge score dimension
5. A result "passes" when `schemaValid === true` and all entries in `checks` are `true`
6. Compares against thresholds from `eval-thresholds.json`
7. Compares against `eval-baseline.json` for regression checks: `pass_rate_drop`, `critical_failures_added` (handle missing `critical` field in baseline by treating those cases as non-critical)
8. Prints a clear summary table to stdout with ✅/❌ indicators
9. Exits with code 0 if all checks pass, code 1 if any hard threshold is violated

The script must handle: missing judge scores (skip judge checks, note "judge scores unavailable"), missing `eval-baseline.json` (skip regression checks, warn), missing `critical` field on baseline results (treat as non-critical), missing eval results directory or empty results (exit with error).

Architecture: Extract core logic into exported functions (`computeMetrics`, `checkThresholds`, `checkRegression`) for unit testability. Use `if (require.main === module) { main(); }` guard pattern. Use CommonJS (`module.exports`) since root `package.json` does not have `"type": "module"`.

Scope: Only create `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js`. Do NOT modify any backend or frontend source files.

steps.title: Split CI into two jobs: eval run (manual/allow_failure) + threshold check (hard-gate), and add npm scripts
steps.description:
Update `.gitlab-ci.yml` to split the eval pipeline into two jobs so deterministic thresholds actually hard-gate:

**Job 1: `eval:llm-judge`** (modify existing) — Keep as `manual`, `allow_failure: true`, image `gradle:8.10.2-jdk21`. It runs `cd backend && ./gradlew --no-daemon evalTest` and produces eval result artifacts. No changes to its script, but ensure its artifacts include `eval-thresholds.json` and `eval-baseline.json` (or the threshold-check job checks them out from the repo root). Since artifacts already include `backend/build/eval-results/`, just keep that.

**Job 2: `eval:threshold-check`** (new job) — Add a NEW job in the `eval` stage that:
- Uses image `node:20-bookworm` (Node.js available natively, no install needed)
- Sets `needs: ["eval:llm-judge"]` so it runs only after the eval completes and receives its artifacts
- Does NOT set `allow_failure: true` (default is false) — so a threshold violation exit code 1 will hard-gate the pipeline
- Has `rules` matching `eval:llm-judge` (i.e., `if: '$OPENROUTER_API_KEY'` with `when: on_success`)
- Script: `node scripts/eval-threshold-check.js`
- The script resolves paths relative to `__dirname`/repo root, so `eval-thresholds.json` and `eval-baseline.json` are read from the checked-out repo, and `backend/build/eval-results/` comes from the `eval:llm-judge` artifacts

This separation means: the LLM call itself can flake without blocking (`allow_failure: true`), but once results exist, threshold violations are enforced (`allow_failure` defaults to `false` on the new job).

Add convenience npm scripts to `package.json`:
- `"eval:check": "node scripts/eval-threshold-check.js"`
- `"eval:run": "cd backend && ./gradlew evalTest"`

Scope: Only modify `.gitlab-ci.yml` and `package.json`. Do NOT modify any backend or frontend source files.

steps.title: Add unit tests for the enforcement script and verify end-to-end
steps.description:
Create `scripts/__tests__/eval-threshold-check.test.js` that tests the threshold check logic with synthetic eval results. The test must `require()` the exported functions from `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js` (see Step 4 architecture). Use Node 20's built-in test runner (`node:test` module) and `node:assert`. Use CommonJS since root package.json does not have `"type": "module"`.

Test cases:
- All passing → no violations
- Schema validity failure → violation reported
- Critical case failure → violation reported
- Regression detected (pass rate drop) → violation reported
- Missing judge scores → skip judge checks, pass on deterministic only
- Missing baseline → skip regression checks, warn

The test should be runnable via `node --test scripts/__tests__/eval-threshold-check.test.js`.

Update `package.json` root `test` script to also run `node --test scripts/__tests__/*.test.js` so the `unit` check picks up these tests. The current `test` script is: `npm run test -w frontend -- --run && npm run test -w @weekly-commit/shared -- --run && cd backend && ./gradlew test`. Append `&& node --test scripts/__tests__/*.test.js` to it.

Scope: Create files in `scripts/__tests__/` and update `package.json`.
